items.blurb: Blushing rounds of foil-kissed colour that dab onto your journal like eyeshadow onto a lid. Palette girls, this one is for you.
Mossy little circles with a foil shimmer, ready to give your planner a quiet forest-walk moment.
Sky-toned dots with a metallic wink, laid out like a beauty palette you can stick anywhere.
Slim rosy bars with foil accents, perfect for underlining the good bits of your week in style.
Buttery yellow bars with a soft shimmer, like highlighter swatches that decided to become stickers.
Deep green bars with foil detailing, for journal spreads that want a grown-up botanical mood.
Cool blue bars with a glint of foil, ideal for calm, airy planner layouts.
Crisp blue triangles with foil edges, an artful geometry lesson for your journal margins.
Forest-toned triangles with a foil sheen, for spreads that lean elegant over sugary.
Mellow yellow triangles with shimmer, scattering sunshine across your pages one point at a time.
Pink triangles with a foil finish, like confetti from the world's most tasteful party.
A wintry daruma on lush Japanese paper, equal parts good-luck charm and mantel decor. Send it or frame it, no wrong answers.
Winter camellias blooming across fine Japanese paper. Proof that snail mail can be the prettiest thing in someone's mailbox.
Two rolls of Spirited Away magic for your journal edges. No-Face approves of every page you decorate.
A Sumikko Gurashi glue stick that goes on blue so you can see your handiwork, then dries invisible. Craft time just got cuter.
A Sumikko Gurashi glue stick that goes on blue so you can see your handiwork, then dries invisible. Craft time just got cuter.
A Rilakkuma glue stick that goes on blue so you can see your handiwork, then dries invisible. Craft time just got cuter.
Twenty sheets of summery Totoro prints begging to become cranes, boxes, or the world's most charming gift wrap.
Springtime Totoro prints in a tidy stack of twenty, ready for folding sessions and impossibly cute wrapping jobs.
Hot-stamped florals that catch the light every time you flip the page. Your journal, but make it golden hour.
A shimmering little meadow in sticker form. Open your planner, catch the light, feel instantly outdoorsy.
A tiny shopping spree rendered in cozy sticker form, for planners that deserve a treat-yourself spread.
Gilded frames that turn any doodle or memo into a small exhibition. Curate your week accordingly.
A whisper-thin roll of shiny pink labels for planners with commitment to the aesthetic. Small tape, big glow-up.
Slim, sunny clear tape that trims planner pages like a ribbon of lemonade. Simple, cheerful, endlessly reusable.
Five envelopes starring Necktie's droll animal characters. Your letters just became the funniest thing in the post.
Chatty animal envelopes by illustrator duo Necktie, five to a set. Pen pals will start replying faster.
A textile designer's animal patterns shrunk into 120 memo sheets. Almost too lovely for grocery lists. Almost.
A wandering cat pads through a snowy Japanese scene on this postcard. Wanderlust, but make it whiskered.
An intrepid postcard cat eyes the autumn fish market. Send it to someone who appreciates a feline with priorities.
Harvest season through the eyes of a traveling cat, painted onto one very giftable postcard.
Mofusand kitties in full Halloween regalia on a postcard sweet enough to skip the candy entirely.
Trick-or-treating mofusand cats crowd this postcard with maximum spooky-cute energy. Boo, but adorable.
Eraser-stamp artist eric doodles her way across San Francisco in this special collab sticker sheet. Cable cars, but cuter.
Five San Francisco scenes stamped into charming wrapping paper by artist eric. Gifts deserve a scenic route.
A pocket notebook dressed in eric's stamp-art, with silky planner paper and a clear pocket for treasures found en route.
mt marks a century of tape-making with three Weisbecker-designed rolls. Collectible washi with genuine art credentials.
Laputa drifts across this Castle in the Sky postcard, ready to carry your words somewhere far above the clouds.
A floating-castle daydream in postcard form. Pin it up or post it, either way it soars.
Nausicaa glides onto a postcard fit for framing. For the friend whose comfort film has an ohmu in it.
A pocket-size letterpress notebook that is really about the paper: two rough, tactile stocks you will want to pet.
Letterpressed stamp motifs you snip out yourself, each one softly debossed by hand in Osaka.
Hand-pressed frames on a cut-your-own sticker sheet, giving every journal entry gallery treatment.
An alphabet gently pressed into sticker paper, for labeling life with letterpress-level dignity.
Moody dark-gray letterpress alphabets on a freeform sheet. Minimalists, your sticker moment has arrived.
Rei Kurahashi's gentle illustration on a card that stands up by itself, moonlighting as desk decor after the occasion.
A palm-size panda card with matching envelope and a foil shimmer. The tiniest, sweetest way to say literally anything.
Princess Mononoke stares down from this postcard with full forest-spirit intensity. Mail with respect.
A dashing pig aviator swoops onto a postcard. Porco Rosso fans, your correspondence just leveled up.
Another chance to send someone the floating city of Laputa, one stamp at a time.
Piglets lounging in peach desserts, per AOYOSHI's okawari series. Second helpings, in sticker form.
Chickens nestled into wobbly puddings, because the okawari series knows exactly what we want.
Bears baked into loaves and buns. AOYOSHI's carb-forward bears may be the coziest stickers in your stash.
A menagerie of little animals curled inside chocolate cornets. Snack-shaped joy for any journal page.
Thirty clear flake stickers of curious stones, like a pocket geology exhibit for your journal.
Seashell specimens on clear flakes, thirty tiny treasures washed straight into your planner.
A blue-tinted cabinet of seashell stickers, thirty pieces of beachcombing without the sand.
Butterflies pinned gently to clear flake stickers, a thirty-piece flutter for green-leaning spreads.
A lilac flock of butterfly stickers, thirty specimens for the softest natural history museum around.
Feather stickers drifting across clear film in warm tones. Thirty light-as-air accents for any page.
Deep navy feathers on clear flakes, thirty moody plumes for journals with a dramatic streak.
Forty-five tracing-paper flakes in orange-leaning everyday motifs, made to layer softly over any journal page.
Forty-five tracing-paper flakes in pink-leaning everyday motifs, made to layer softly over any journal page.
Faux wax-seal stickers in golden tones: all the old-world romance of sealing letters, none of the melting.
Faux wax-seal stickers in crimson tones: all the old-world romance of sealing letters, none of the melting.
A skinny roll of shiny mixed motifs that dresses up planners, jars, and presents in one glinting pass.
Keina Higashide's abstract pieces shimmer along this clear tape, part washi, part wearable art for your notebooks.
Wide clear tape floating with HOHOEMI's balloons: one strip and any page, jar, or gift starts drifting skyward.
A handcrafted brass clip from Okayama that hugs your notebook cover and ages beautifully. Heirloom-grade desk jewelry.
Rubber-stamp-style kitchen doodles in sticker form, for meal plans and recipe journals with charm to spare.
Stamped-look flower stickers that scatter a hand-pressed garden across your planner in seconds.
Twenty washi flakes from a candy-cottage world, printed on fine Mino paper and gone when they're gone.
A mini letter set from the Sweets House collection: washi paper notes sweet enough to tuck into any little gift.
One hundred memo sheets of confectionery-cottage designs. To-do lists were never meant to be this delicious.
Rakusui washi with a raindrop texture and dried-flower delicacy, folded into a mini letter set for slow, lovely mail.
Squirrels and chestnuts scamper across this soebumi-sen mini letter set, made for slipping a warm note into anything.
A bear among apples on Mino washi: thirty mini sheets and five envelopes for notes with orchard-level warmth.
Fifty-four romantic flake stickers by Milina, foil-touched and ready to make every page a little love letter.
Sweet animal flakes with a foil sparkle, fifty-four pieces of gentle chaos for planners and gift tags alike.
Yoko's fluffy animals parade down this washi tape with gold-stamped accents. Warning: pages may become 90% fluff.
